tldr: At the headline layer, Gabbard's resignation is framed almost universally as a straightforward Trump administration personnel change. Only Globe and Mail names the husband's cancer in the headline itself; India-based outlets uniquely use their headline slots to question that explanation (OpIndia) or draw factional conclusions (Hindustan Times). Daily Wire foregrounds forthcoming intelligence disclosures; Mother Jones pivots entirely to the right-wing conspiracy ecosystem's response. Five of seven country-buckets offer no distinctive frame beyond the bare personnel announcement.

frames.sub_frame: DNI exit as Trump admin personnel event

evidence.quote: Trump Reacts To Tulsi Gabbard's Surprise Resignation
Trump's national intelligence chief Gabbard announces
Diretora de Inteligência Nacional dos EUA renuncia
Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's director
Tulsi Gabbard's exit weakens MAGA's anti-war faction
US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard resigns
What Tulsi Gabbard Plans To Reveal Before Leaving Office
fell out with Trump over the Iran war, Venezuela operation

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet whose headline is primarily about the right-wing conspiracy ecosystem's response rather than the resignation itself — the resignation is framed as backdrop to an online reaction story, inverting the conventional announcement structure.

Only headline to frame the departure as an intelligence disclosure event ('Plans To Reveal Before Leaving Office') rather than a personnel change or health emergency — the story is what she will do next, not that she is leaving.

Uses 'quits' rather than 'resigns' — the only outlet to choose vocabulary with an adversarial/impulsive connotation, subtly implying rupture without stating political disagreement; all other English-language outlets use 'resigns' or 'announces resignation.'

Only outlet to publish a question headline explicitly challenging the cancer explanation and naming Iran war and Venezuela operations as alternative causes — the policy-disagreement hypothesis is in the headline itself, not buried in the body.

Publishes a tabloid gossip headline ('bizarre nickname,' 'explosive claim') foregrounding intra-MAGA hostility from Trump allies — a completely distinct story angle absent from every other outlet's headline.

bottom_line: Headline coverage reduces Gabbard's exit to a bare personnel-change announcement across most country-buckets; only India-based outlets and US ideological-outlier titles use the headline slot to advance skeptical, factional, or intelligence-disclosure frames that diverge sharply from the official stated reason.
